digest.greeting: Jean, today closed the Universal Intake follow-up and surfaced a cleaner path for the remaining operating work. The open list is mostly about keeping PM structure, BD transition, intake routing, Mental Health, LA AI testing, Legal Change Management, and AmazonConnect/Venn from splitting into parallel tracks.

items.label: Closed the Universal Intake next-step item
items.detail: The Universal Intake follow-up is closed for today after Matthew's thread added Jean as the BD project manager and identified the need for a meeting before UI changes move forward.

items.label: Turn the PM team index, Planner, weekly deck, and open-thread follow-ups into one operating structure
items.detail: Keep shaping the Loop index, Planner updates, weekly executive deck, and EPMO TL follow-ups into one operating rhythm with clear owners and dates.

items.label: Align BD portfolio review with Janet's transition touchpoint
items.detail: Keep the BD review, transition path, active work, risks, new intake, owner decisions, and transition next steps connected as one operating lane.

items.label: Review the new intake alerts before portfolio work
items.detail: Keep the Phase 2 and BD intake items routed to ownership or portfolio follow-up before they fragment across separate tracking paths.

items.label: Check the Mental Health Expansion charter deliverable
items.detail: Confirm whether the draft charter is ready for stakeholder review or needs an owner/date reset.

items.label: Start LA AI change-management testing
items.detail: Give the LA AI testing item a clean first pass now that several heavier July 13 follow-ups are closed.

items.label: Confirm the Legal Change Management follow-up path after the 11:00 AM conflict
items.detail: The IT/EPMO sync overlapped Jean's protected block. Capture any decision points, delegated follow-up, or next steps so the Legal Change Management path stays clear.

items.label: Prepare the AmazonConnect/Venn OCM risk view
items.detail: Sara's invite framed the meeting as a high-priority deep dive. Keep the timeline, stakeholder alignment, blockers, OCM risks, and mitigation path together after the discussion.

items.label: Latent Variables pilot testing will use champion links for now
items.detail: Hector's follow-up says IT whitelisting is not in place yet, so the practical test path is to use champion links manually with a small 3-5 person group across functions before showing Sarah and Danny the end-to-end workflow.

items.label: Shelley remains OOO
items.detail: Shelley's calendar shows her out through Friday, July 17. Route anything needing her around that absence.

items.label: Sara remains in the Dublin office
items.detail: Sara's calendar shows her in the Dublin office through Thursday.

items.label: Legal Change Management - IT & EPMO Sync
items.detail: Another Legal Change Management sync is on tomorrow's calendar; use today's follow-up to clarify whether this one is still needed.

items.label: Marketing Projects Portfolio Review and Claude overlap
items.detail: The Marketing portfolio review and Claude session both sit at 11:00 AM, so tomorrow already has a prioritization decision.

items.label: Jean/Sara 1:1 entries are canceled tomorrow
items.detail: Two Jean/Sara 1:1 calendar entries show as canceled while Sara is in Dublin.

items.label: 1:1 Maria & Jean
items.detail: Portfolio review, roadblocks, and next steps. Mar accepted the invite today.

items.label: PM Open Discussion: How to avoid going sideways on projects?
items.detail: Use today's PM operating-structure work to shape a cleaner discussion tomorrow.
